title: Listening - Part 4: Transitioning to Sustainable Lifestyles
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people who adopted sustainable lifestyles for varying reasons. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I've always been conscious of the environmental crisis, but it was only after I noticed [21]the impact my own habits were having[/21] that I decided to change. Switching to a zero-waste lifestyle seemed daunting at first, but the realisation that even small actions matter kept me motivated. I got involved with a local zero-waste community, which provided immense support and practical tips. It's been an interesting journey, balancing the challenges of managing waste with a more minimalist approach to life. What I value most now is [26]the sense of purpose and fulfillment[/26] I derive from knowing I'm part of the solution, not the problem. || Speaker 2: Initially, what drove me was [22]a deep concern about the chemicals used in conventional products[/22]. I developed an interest in making my own, from cleaning products to personal care. It all started with wanting a healthier environment for my children, but it gradually evolved into a wider lifestyle change. I now spend a lot of time researching and sharing my findings online, which has unexpectedly connected me with like-minded individuals who were once strangers. The [27]sense of community and shared goals[/27] has been so rewarding and motivating. || Speaker 3: The hypocrisy I felt as an environmental activist, yet my lifestyle was anything but sustainable, finally got to me. So, I decided to [23]drastically reduce my carbon footprint[/23]. I began by implementing strict energy-saving measures at home, using solar panels, and biking wherever I could instead of driving. The learning curve was steep, but it suited my passion for self-discipline and innovation. I often ended up devising my own solutions, which was quite fulfilling, and the most rewarding part has been [28]seeing tangible reductions in my energy consumption[/28] and costs, proving lifestyle choices can indeed make a difference. || Speaker 4: Watching a documentary on ocean pollution brought realisation home. It made me see that I was inadvertently contributing to the problem. I decided to adopt a [24]plastic-free lifestyle[/24]. At first, it was incredibly challenging to change my shopping habits, but gradually, it became easier. Now, I prefer buying bulk goods and using cloth bags. It's hard to believe how much plastic we use unnecessarily. Knowing that my choices now help protect marine life, rather than harm it, is incredibly fulfilling. [29]Making an impact that directly aids wildlife[/29] is what I cherish most. || Speaker 5: For me, it was seeing [25]the wastefulness of fast fashion[/25] that triggered my shift to a more sustainable lifestyle. I started by creating a capsule wardrobe, which forced me to think deeply about my clothing choices. I also learned to sew and mend my own clothes, which turned out to be a creative outlet I never knew I needed. The sense of satisfaction I get from repurposing old garments and making them useful again is unmatched. Plus, [30]living with less clutter and more creativity[/30] has brought a surprising amount of joy into my life.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) why each speaker decided to make a change.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 decided to change upon realizing the environmental impact of his habits.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 was motivated by concerns about chemicals in conventional products.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 aimed to reduce his carbon footprint due to his environmental beliefs.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 was influenced by seeing a documentary on ocean pollution.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5's change was prompted by the wastefulness of fast fashion.

options.A: Concern about the wastefulness of fast fashion
options.B: Desire to decrease personal carbon output
options.D: Impact from a documentary on pollution

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker finds most rewarding about their new life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 finds the sense of purpose and fulfillment rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values the sense of community and shared goals.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 finds the tangible reductions in energy consumption rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 cherishes making an impact that aids wildlife.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 enjoys living with less clutter and more creativity.
